metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` as an explicit edit target (soften 'remediate findings before claiming' to judgment-based framing), and broaden the AC3 negative-grep regex to match this phrasing (e.g., add `|remediate findings before claiming` to the pattern).
findings.finding: examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135 contains an unqualified mandatory-remediation echo ('remediate findings before claiming') that is not listed as an edit target in the plan's 'What' section, and the AC3 negative-proof grep regex ('remediate every valid finding|remediate all valid advisor') would not match this phrasing — so the echo would survive the change and pass the AC3 proof undetected.
findings.location: What section (sweep scope) and AC3 negative grep; examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add: 'Also soften `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` — change "remediate findings before claiming" to "apply the advisory findings that genuinely improve the plan before claiming; remediate any blocking finding".' Update AC3 negative grep to: `grep -rniE "remediate every valid finding|remediate all valid advisor|remediate findings before claiming" CLAUDE.md docs/ CONTRIBUTING.md examples/`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the sweep to explicitly assess `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` ('remediate findings before claiming') and, if it could reasonably be read as mandating advisory remediation, soften it to judgment framing; update AC3's negative grep pattern to cover this phrasing or add a separate proof line for it.
findings.finding: The plan's sweep of 'examples/' identifies only one known echo (rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136 — 'remediate every valid finding'), but rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135 also contains advisory-remediation language ('remediate findings before claiming') that won't be matched by the AC3 negative grep pattern ('remediate every valid finding|remediate all valid advisor'). This second echo in a committed skill copy may survive the change unchecked, leaving agents reading the brainstorm skill still guided toward mandatory remediation.
findings.location: What section — 'Sweep for echoes' paragraph; AC3 negative grep; examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` to the list of known echoes. In AC3, broaden the negative grep to: `grep -rniE 'remediate every valid finding|remediate all valid advisor|remediate findings before' <those paths>` (or add a separate positive-proof line confirming the brainstorm skill's phrasing is judgment-framed).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the 'What' section whether `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` ('remediate findings before claiming') is in scope for softening, and if so extend AC3's negative-grep pattern to cover it (or add a separate positive-proof grep for its replacement text).
findings.finding: The plan's sweep scope names only `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136` as the 'known echo,' but `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` contains the phrase 'remediate findings before claiming' — an undifferentiated statement that does not distinguish blocking from advisory findings, carrying the same potential mandatory-advisory-remediation instruction as the named echo. The plan's negative-grep patterns (`remediate every valid finding|remediate all valid advisor`) would NOT match this line, so AC3's negative-proof grep would pass while the brainstorm skill still carries ambiguous mandatory-remediation framing. The plan provides no decision on whether this echo is in-scope for softening.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' sweep: 'Also review `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135` — the phrase "remediate findings before claiming" is ambiguous (does not distinguish advisory from blocking); soften it to "remediate any blocking finding before claiming; apply advisory findings at your judgment" to match the judgment-based framing.' Extend AC3's negative grep to include `remediate findings before claiming` or add a positive-proof grep for the replacement sentence.

findings.finding: The plan's sweep scope and AC3 grep regex do not cover examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135, which contains 'remediate findings before claiming' — an unqualified echo that, unlike the named targets, does not say 'all/every valid' but could still be read by agents as requiring full remediation of all advisory findings before claiming.
findings.location: What section (sweep scope) and AC3 (proving grep); gap is at examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135
findings.suggested_fix: Change SKILL.md:135 from 'remediate findings before claiming' to 'review and apply findings before claiming' (or similar judgment framing), and broaden the AC3 grep to also check `grep -rniE 'remediate (all|every|the)? ?findings' examples/` to catch unqualified instances.

findings.finding: The AC3 grep proving command ('grep -rniE "remediate (all|every) valid|must remediate all" CLAUDE.md docs/ CONTRIBUTING.md examples/') will NOT match the rebar-brainstorm echo at examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135 ('remediate findings before claiming'), producing a false-negative: the AC passes green while a mandate-adjacent echo in an agent-facing skill doc remains unchecked. The plan says to sweep ALL agent-facing docs under examples/ but the verification pattern is too narrow to confirm completeness.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second proving command: `grep -rniE 'remediate' examples/claude-skills/ | grep -viE 'BLOCK|fail|retry|completion'` to surface any residual remediate-findings phrasing in skill docs, and explicitly address rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135 in the What section's sweep list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive grep proving command to AC3 that verifies SKILL.md still contains its blocking-verdict instruction after the edit (e.g., `grep -n "remediate any blocking finding and re-run until it passes" examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` returns a match).
findings.finding: SKILL.md's BLOCK-must-remediate / re-run-until-pass instruction is required to be retained (per the What section and AC3 prose), but no proving command verifies its presence after the edit — the AC3 positive grep only checks for the new judgment-based framing sentence, not the blocking clause.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3, positive-side grep for examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: '(retention proof) `grep -n "remediate any blocking finding and re-run until it passes" examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` returns the retained instruction.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that 'change 562' (convergence floors default-ON) is actually landed: add a grep/file reference (e.g. the config key, a test, or a changelog entry) that confirms this precondition exists in the repo before the loosening lands.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the convergence floors now default-ON (change 562)' as a factual premise motivating the loosening, but no file in the repository contains 'convergence floors' or 'change 562'. This is an unverifiable codebase assertion — the referenced change cannot be confirmed as landed or as having made convergence floors default-ON.
findings.location: Why section: 'With the convergence floors now default-ON (change 562) the operator wants to LOOSEN this guidance'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Why section, replace 'the convergence floors now default-ON (change 562)' with a verifiable reference — e.g. cite the specific config key or test that proves floors are default-ON, or add an AC that confirms 'change 562 is merged' as a precondition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out pointer to the plan: either state the revert condition inline (e.g., 'if advisory-uptake rate regresses past threshold X, revert this commit') or add an AC that verifies the parent epic's rollback criterion covers this change.
findings.finding: The plan removes a behavioral mandate from agent-facing docs (a change to existing agent behavior) but specifies no rollback condition or back-out path — the observation plan is entirely delegated to parent epic 9d50 AC2, which is not referenced here. T4 requires more than acknowledgement; an explicit rollback/revert trigger (e.g., 'if advisory-uptake rate drops below baseline, revert this change') or a pointer to where that condition lives is needed.
findings.location: What section (last bullet) and ACs overall
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the What section: 'If the measurement framework on epic 9d50 AC2 shows advisory-uptake regression past the agreed threshold, this change is reverted by restoring the removed MUST-remediate language in CLAUDE.md and SKILL.md.' Optionally add a corresponding AC: `grep -n 'revert' epic-9d50` or a cross-reference AC that confirms the parent epic's back-out plan covers this commit.

findings.finding: AC1 and AC3 each assert a POSITIVE property (replacement text is 'judgment-based and does not discourage acting on advisories'; SKILL.md is 'softened to judgment framing') but provide no proving command for that positive claim — only the REMOVAL of the mandate is verifiable via the grep in AC3. An agent could delete the mandate and insert neutral or discouraging text and still pass all provided commands.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('the replacement framing is judgment-based and does not discourage acting on advisories') and AC3 ('examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md is softened to judgment framing')
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: Proving command: `grep -niE 'judgment|genuinely improve|apply.*improve' CLAUDE.md` must return at least one match in the edited paragraph. Append to AC3: `grep -niE 'judgment|genuinely improve' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` must return at least one match in the softened sentence.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
